transcript.text: Hi, this is a bike tour blog, May 31st, 2010.
I rode down from South Sarajevo today.
The idea was to ride from Sarajevo to Podgorica, but I slept in again.
I stayed in Sarajevo three nights, and I stayed the third night because I couldn't get up in the morning.
Well, I could have gotten up and checked out by 10 and everything, but I was like, man, fuck this.
I'm just going to stay here again because freaking, I want to stay up.
I mean, I want to get up early and ride my bike from freaking early from like five, man, so I can get all the way to Podgorica tomorrow.
But today I slept in again like I cannot get up in the morning to save my life, even if I'm totally freaking like fresh.
Like today I was up, I was, I was awake, but I was just laying there because, well, there's that chance I might be able to dream and stuff,
And I'm more relaxed, and it's just nice to sleep, just to be so relaxed in the morning.
And that's another video, though, but anyway, I didn't get out until, I didn't start rolling from the hostel until like 9.38 in the morning,
Which is pretty early actually for me for this trip.
I think it's like the second earliest that I've gotten going, or at least that I can remember on this trip.
But 8.30 is the earliest that I remember actually getting going.
So, but alas, it wasn't early enough to make it to Podgorica in one day.
I'm 50 kilometers outside of Podgorica, and before it got dark and I always had to get a hotel.
I could have tried to do the internet and find out, had dinner, and then gone and camped,
But I don't really know what it's like between here.
I'm in a town called like Nityazilic or something like that, and it's a big town, and I don't know what.
I don't know in a country called Montenegro, and it could be like totally populated between here and there,
And I didn't want to deal with trying to find a place in the pitch black.
I don't have like proper lighting on my bike.
I can't really ride at night, and it was raining.
It was dumping cats and dogs, man, when I got to this hotel, dumping.
And it was pretty much raining all day.
The first couple hours it wasn't raining, but then it started raining.
It was raining all day.
But today probably was the most beautiful day that I've done.
And the whole bike tour, it was breathtaking.
I mean, the entire day.
Like I got like from Sarajevo, I was going up hills, and it was really awesome.
I loved it.
There was like hardly any traffic, and I was going up hills, but it wasn't like totally steep.
It was like a nice pleasant climb.
And then I think it was a small chainring climb, but it was a nice climb, and it was like beautiful like jungle all over the place.
And then I saw the ski runs coming right off of like the mountain, like above Timberline Mountain.
It looked like Colorado.
And then I went up from Sarajevo, it was 800 feet, and then I went up to like 4,100 feet or something.
And then it dipped down again to like 1,800 feet again.
I forgot to do the how much feet I gained and lost today.
I forgot to push the button.

And then I went, and then I got to a valley where I was going up this river, and for like a bunch of kilometers, like 20 or something, I was riding alongside this river like way up on the cliff, like really steep cliff, on a tiny, tiny little road with no traffic, like a bike path-sized road, almost.
And along like the side of this like really steep ravine.
And then I dipped down, and I crossed the river into the new country, from Bosnia-Herzegovina to Montenegro.
And then I rode up, and then it was through this really intense canyon, lined by cliffs, through a bunch of tunnels.
And then I went across this bridge, it was like 200 feet up, and then up a road.
Like through like more, like a bunch more tunnels, and then across the reservoir, and then like I climbed more.
Or I was going alongside this huge lake that was like 20 kilometers long or something, and it kept going, you know.
Like after I climbed up the hill where it split, I went across the river to the other side of it.
But I went through so many tunnels.
I went through like tons of really big tunnels, and one that was like a kilometer long and was totally pitch black, and I couldn't see anything.
I didn't feel, I couldn't feel myself moving, and I didn't, had no idea if I was like right on the side about to hit the, you know, hit the side of the tunnel.
Like went out of whatever side.
And so I was going really slowly, but because I found out like my light was totally dead because somehow it died.
I guess it got too wet or cold or something.
But um, and then I crossed the river to the other side where it split, this lake, this reservoir.
And so it was actually really steep mountains with snow on them that were kind of mostly hidden, though, from the clouds.
And then, you know, because it was raining really hard on them, not raining.
Like I took my rain jacket on and off like 12 times today.
And then I went over, and then I got to this little village town, which is beautiful, and then went up and climbed up again away from the reservoir up to about 40, 100 feet.
And then dipped down into where I am now, which is, which is, uh, 2390.
And, um, oh my God.
And, um, well, I'm 50 kilometers outside of Podgarica, but Podgarica is right, Podgarica is right on the ocean.
But I think I read in Wikipedia something that's like 400 feet, because it's like right next to the ocean.
Which means I'm going to dip, I'm going to drop 1,900 feet in 15, in 30 miles.
So that's cool.
I just realized that now.
But, um, yeah, there was like lots of rolling hills, like up and down, up and down, up and down the entire day.
And there were lots of sections where the road was really bad.
And all those tunnels, I went through like 100 tunnels today.
Like long tunnels where you have to like go really slowly, because you can't see.
So anyways, in about 10 and a half hours of riding, I only made 106 miles.
But, um, if I had started earlier, like 730 instead of 930, like 730 like I intended on, I would have been able to make it to Podgarica today.
And I would have been spending 10 euros for a hotel instead of 37.
But I didn't want to just go and find a place to camp in this rain.
And, you know, I didn't even know like what it's like between here and there.
I don't know.
I get a free breakfast.
So anyways, whatever.
I'm just weak minded.
But, uh, I wasn't really riding very hard today either because, um, because, um, wow, eight minutes.
Because, um, it was so cold.
Like, it was like rainy.
Like, I wasn't cold, but my legs were cold.
And I just wasn't riding hard.
And I took two days off.
And before that, I took, I rode for two days.
And then before that, I took two full days off.
Or was it three full days off?
It was four nights.
So it was three full days off.
So I haven't been riding much.
And I was feeling like I, I, I didn't have any energy too.
Because all I ate yesterday was this one helping of that special strudel thing that they have.
And then I didn't eat anything until I have pizza at the end of the day.
So I could feel like my energy was down.
But I was eating a lot during the day, during today.
I ate like a half kilo of cherries, a half kilo of, uh, of strawberries, a half kilo of bananas.
And a bunch of granola bars, two big pizzas of pizza.
And, uh, yeah.
And that kept me going.
And actually, by the end of the day, when I rolled into here, I felt awesome, dude.
I felt like I could have gone like another 12 hours.
Like I could have ridden.
I felt like I could have, today, physically, would have been the day to do the 24-hour ride.
And I could have done it.
But, um, and, and I felt like when I came into the room, I felt like my legs felt like they didn't, I didn't even go on a ride.
Or my body, I didn't feel like I went on a ride at all.
And then I had some dinner, and then I walked up the stairs, and I did feel a little bit of a burn, but it's not that much.
So, even though I took like five days off in the last week, um, I'm stronger now than I was.
Um, so, so I think I'm going to, tomorrow, I'm just going to ride into Podgarica, which is only 30 miles away.
It's like 50 kilometers away.
That's what they told me.
And then, um, stay there tomorrow night.
And then tomorrow I'll do, I'll do my, like, tour around Podgarica, get to know Podgarica, capital city of Montenegro.
And then, day after tomorrow I'll leave.
So I'll only spend one night in Podgarica.
And I think I'll be able to, I'll just try to get up really early the next day.
If I can get up really early the next day.
Well, we'll see how far it is to the next capital city.
But if it's kind of far, I'll see if I can, uh, just get up super early and ride there in one day.
Um, I could have made it to Podgarica today if it had have been, like, normal conditions.
Oh, it was also really windy, man.
Like, headwinds from fucking hell.
It was insane.
Like, 40 miles an hour.
Anyways, I'm out of time.
